title: Reinforcement Learning
subtitle: RL agents built from scratch - explore the code and play each one live

projects.title: Q-Learning Tic Tac Toe
projects.summary: tabular Q-learning -> dict[(state, action)] -> Bellman update
projects.description: A tabular Q-learner that picks moves via epsilon-greedy over a dict mapping (state, action) to estimated value. Trained against a uniformly-random opponent for 10,000 episodes. States are frozen 3x3 board snapshots; actions are (row, col) coordinates.
bullets: alpha=0.1 learning rate - how aggressively new estimates override old
gamma=0.9 discount - values future reward 0.9x per step
epsilon=0.1 exploration - 1 in 10 moves is random, keeping coverage alive
1134 (state, action) pairs in the Q-table - full coverage of reachable positions

projects.title: Q-Learning Tic Tac Toe (Self-Play)
projects.summary: tabular Q-learning -> two agents play each other -> Bellman update
projects.description: Two tabular Q-learners trained against each other via self-play. Both use epsilon-greedy with linear decay from 1.0 to 0.05. After each move, the opponent's prior (state, action) is updated with the resulting next-board so both sides learn from every non-terminal step.
bullets: self-play - two Q-tables, each learning against the other
